model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, batch size directly controls how many training examples the model sees before evaluation. Increasing from 64 to 256 lets this compact CNN cover the full 50k training set within the budget while preserving the current optimizer and schedule, making it a strong low-complexity next experiment.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is still very shallow. Adding a third pooling block increases feature hierarchy and receptive field while actually shrinking the classifier input, so this is a compact architecture upgrade with limited parameter growth and a reasonable runtime risk under the current batch-256 setup.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper 3-block network is working, and its channel widths are still conservative. Raising the base width from 12 to 16 is a simple capacity increase that should strengthen learned features without changing the training loop or introducing extra optimization complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent gains suggest the model is still capacity-limited. A moderate width bump from 16 to 20 keeps the same successful training recipe and architecture shape while giving each stage more representational power, which is a strong next step before trying more invasive optimization changes.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The convolutional trunk has been strengthened substantially, while the MLP head is still narrow. Doubling the hidden size is a low-risk capacity increase with little impact on convolutional compute, so it is a good next test now that the feature extractor is much stronger.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity increases have been the most reliable source of improvement so far, and the current configuration still uses a relatively modest width for a 3-block CIFAR CNN. A moderate bump from 20 to 24 preserves the successful recipe while testing whether the model remains width-limited at the current fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, batch size still controls how much effective training the model gets. Moving from 256 to 384 should let the current stronger architecture cover nearly two full passes over CIFAR-10 within the same step budget, which is a promising next lever now that width and depth have improved substantially.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The batch-size increase substantially changed the optimization scale, but the learning rate stayed fixed. A moderate LR increase to 1.5e-3 is a targeted follow-up that should better match the larger batch and stronger model while remaining conservative given the existing warmup and cosine decay.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous LR increase produced another clear improvement, which suggests the current large-batch setup is still under-driving optimization. Raising the base rate to 2e-3 is the most direct continuation of that successful trend while keeping the same warmup and decay safeguards.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Learning-rate increases have continued to pay off in the current large-batch regime, indicating optimization is still not saturated. A further moderate step to 2.5e-3 is the cleanest continuation of that trend while preserving the warmup-plus-cosine schedule that has kept training stable.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The current recipe continues to improve as effective sample coverage per fixed 256-step run increases. Raising batch size from 384 to 512 is a clean next test that should expose the model to substantially more of CIFAR-10 within the same optimizer-step budget, while keeping the successful architecture, optimizer, and schedule unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model improved again after increasing batch size, but the base learning rate was not retuned for the larger optimization scale. Raising it from 2.5e-3 to 3e-3 is a focused next step that matches the established large-batch trend while retaining the same warmup and cosine safeguards.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current large-batch, high-learning-rate setup appears to be approaching diminishing returns from pure LR scaling. Switching from Adam to AdamW and strengthening weight decay is a compact optimizer-family change that can improve generalization by decoupling regularization from the adaptive update dynamics without disrupting the successful schedule or architecture.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current model is now much larger and trains for multiple effective passes through the data within the fixed step budget, so a small amount of dropout is a reasonable low-cost regularization test. Setting dropout to 0.1 applies light regularization to both the convolutional blocks and classifier without changing the core optimization recipe.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The added dropout caused a large regression, so the safest high-value move is to remove it and restore the stronger no-dropout setting. That keeps the beneficial AdamW configuration while undoing the only change that clearly hurt validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Most of the strongest gains in this run came from increasing model capacity, and the current optimizer/schedule/batch recipe is already well-tuned. A moderate width increase from 24 to 28 is the most promising remaining simple change to test whether the model is still capacity-limited under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity increases have been the most reliable source of improvement throughout this run, and the latest width bump was again positive. With only one step remaining, the highest-upside simple move is to push width once more from 28 to 32 and test whether the model is still capacity-limited under the current strong optimizer and batch regime.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}
